intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is severely compromised. P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of foliage. P3 (Black Pot) contains the scale anchor (white rabbit) but shows no viable plant matter. P2 and P4 are indistinguishable or absent from the visible frame.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains dark, undisturbed soil. No green biomass detected.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains the white rabbit figurine. Soil appears dark and damp, but no Pothos foliage is present.
plant_audit.P2_P4: No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest in the provided frame.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all visible pots.
biome_observations.debris: A metal spoon is introduced in the T-1 frame, resting in or near P1. No fungal growth detected, but the lack of vegetation suggests a dormant or failed substrate.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: No significant change; pots appear empty.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t1: Introduction of a metal spoon near P1. No plant growth observed.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Removal of the spoon. The biome remains in a state of total vegetative failure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total vegetative collapse. There is no evidence of living plant tissue in any of the pots. The presence of the white rabbit anchor in an empty pot confirms the loss of the Pothos specimen.
anomalies: New Introduction: Metal spoon (T-1, removed by Current).
Systemic Loss: All registered plant specimens (P1-P4) are absent or dead.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of the expected botanical specimens. The environment is maintained (damp soil), but the biological components have been lost. The white rabbit remains as a static marker in an empty pot. The brief appearance of a spoon suggests human intervention, but it has not resulted in any restorative growth.
